evidences.content: The Wild Card round features six games — three within the NFC, and three within the AFC — between 12 of the NFL’s 14 playoff teams. The two teams that don’t play during Wild Card weekend represent the top-seeded teams from the regular season who earned both a first-round bye as well as home-field advantage within their conference for the duration of their stay in the NFL Playoffs. This year, the No. 1-seeded teams are the Seattle Seahawks (NFC) and the Denver Broncos (AFC). The rest of the Wild Card bracket (Seeds 2-7) is determined by a two-tiered pecking order: Seeds 2-4 represent teams that won their Division within each conference, while Seeds 5-7 represent the top remaining teams from each conference that have the best regular season record (with tie breaks factored in).
